page: Amazon Web Services d3.2xlarge is a dense storage instance built on the Nitro hypervisor. It features an x86_64 architecture powered by an Intel Xeon 8259CL processor operating at 3.1 GHz. The instance provides 8 vCPUs, consisting of 4 physical cores with hyper-threading enabled, and 64.0 GB of DDR4 memory running at 3 GHz. A key hardware characteristic is its massive local storage capacity, consisting of 11,880 GB of local HDD storage. Network performance is supported by a 6 Gbps baseline bandwidth.

Benchmark data indicates that the d3.2xlarge delivers average multi-core CPU performance, with a stress-ng multi-core score of 6744.33 and a PassMark CPU score of 7914.79. However, single-core performance is relatively weak, as shown by a Geekbench single-core score of 1117.0 and a stress-ng single-core score of 1284.58. Memory bandwidth is average, reaching 51.95 GB/sec for large-block read operations and 41.01 GB/sec for large-block write operations. Database operations and web serving throughput also fall into the average tier, with Redis SET operations achieving 6.8 million operations per second and static web serving throughput reaching 6.98 GB/sec. LLM inference performance is weak to poor for medium-sized 7B models, though it handles small 135M models at average speeds.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the d3.2xlarge offers a high density of local HDD storage relative to its compute and memory resources, making it a specialized option for storage-heavy workloads rather than compute-intensive tasks. Given its massive local storage capacity and average database performance, this instance is best suited for workloads such as large-scale data warehousing, distributed file systems, MapReduce jobs, and high-capacity database storage.
description: Amazon Web Services d3.2xlarge is a dense storage instance utilizing the Nitro hypervisor and an x86_64 Intel Xeon 8259CL processor running at 3.1 GHz. It is configured with 8 vCPUs, 64.0 GB of DDR4 memory, and a baseline network bandwidth of 6 Gbps. The defining feature of this instance is its massive 11,880 GB of local HDD storage. Performance benchmarks show average multi-core CPU capabilities, with a PassMark CPU score of 7914.79, alongside average memory bandwidth and database performance, such as 6.8 million Redis SET operations per second. However, single-core compute performance is weak. The high ratio of local storage to compute resources makes the d3.2xlarge highly cost-efficient for storage-dense applications. It is best suited for data warehousing, distributed file systems, and large-scale database workloads requiring high-capacity local storage.
og_description: Amazon Web Services d3.2xlarge is a dense storage instance featuring 8 vCPUs, 64 GB RAM, and 11,880 GB of local HDD storage, optimized for data warehousing and large-scale database workloads.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services d3.2xlarge is a dense storage instance with 8 vCPUs, 64 GB RAM, and 11,880 GB of local HDD storage on the Nitro hypervisor.
tagline: A dense storage instance featuring massive local HDD capacity and balanced memory for large-scale data warehousing and database workloads.
bullet_points: Powered by an 8-vCPU Intel Xeon 8259CL processor at 3.1 GHz
Equipped with 64.0 GB of DDR4 memory and 6 Gbps baseline network bandwidth
Features a massive 11,880 GB of local HDD storage
Delivers average multi-core CPU and database performance
Best suited for dense storage workloads, data warehousing, and distributed file systems
